content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Remediated the adapter test suites without touching production adapter code. `tests/unit/br_adapter_test.rs` now covers nominal dependency mutations for `add_dependency`, explicit `parent-child` adds, and `remove_dependency`, asserting command-line construction plus pending-mutation journaling/dirty-state behavior. `tests/unit/bv_adapter_test.rs` now distinguishes the empty-graph and fully-blocked `bv --robot-next` no-work scenarios with separate scripted payloads so the blocked-graph test adds real coverage instead of duplicating the empty-graph case.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the new BR/BV adapter test files against the pending amendments and confirm the missing BR dependency-mutation coverage plus the duplicated BV no-work scenario.
2. [Completed] Patch `tests/unit/br_adapter_test.rs` to add happy-path dependency mutation tests for default `dep add`, explicit `--type=parent-child`, and `dep remove`, asserting command args and pending-mutation journal/dirty-state behavior.
3. [Completed] Patch `tests/unit/bv_adapter_test.rs` so the blocked-graph `--robot-next` case uses a distinct structured blocked payload instead of duplicating the empty-graph fixture, then rerun focused and full verification including `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and `nix build`.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo test --test unit br_adapter` passed with 19/19 BR adapter tests green after adding dependency-mutation coverage.
- `cargo test --test unit bv_adapter` passed with all 8 BV adapter tests green after differentiating the blocked-graph fixture.
- `cargo test` passed on the full tree.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passed.
- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `nix build` passed on the final tree.

## Outstanding Risks

- Verification mutated live orchestration state under `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni46-1/`; those files were intentionally left untouched per the prompt’s explicit exclusion of `.ralph-burning/` from review scope.
- `nix build` refreshed the tracked `result` symlink in the worktree, and the differentiated blocked-graph BV fixture is test-local; if upstream `bv --robot-next` standardizes a different blocked payload, that fixture will need to be updated.
